act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Definitions
content: This section contains over one hundred definitions essential to the operation of the GST regime. Key definitions include: 'aggregate turnover' means the aggregate value of all taxable supplies, exempt supplies, exports of goods or services, and inter-State supplies of persons having the same PAN, computed on an all-India basis, excluding the value of inward supplies on which tax is payable on reverse charge basis and CGST, SGST, UTGST, IGST, and cess; 'business' includes any trade, commerce, manufacture, profession, vocation, adventure, wager, or any other similar activity; 'casual taxable person' means a person who occasionally undertakes transactions in the course of business in a State where he has no fixed place of business; 'goods' means every kind of movable property other than money and securities but includes actionable claims; 'services' means anything other than goods, money, and securities; 'supply' includes all forms of supply of goods or services such as sale, transfer, barter, exchange, licence, rental, lease, or disposal.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Scope of Supply
content: The expression 'supply' includes all forms of supply of goods or services or both such as sale, transfer, barter, exchange, licence, rental, lease, or disposal made or agreed to be made for a consideration by a person in the course or furtherance of business. It includes import of services for a consideration whether or not in the course or furtherance of business, and the activities specified in Schedule I made or agreed to be made without a consideration. Schedule I includes permanent transfer or disposal of business assets, supply of goods or services between related persons or between distinct persons, supply of goods by a principal to his agent or vice versa, and import of services from a related person or an establishment of the same person outside India. Activities listed in Schedule III are treated as neither supply of goods nor supply of services.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Levy and Collection
content: There shall be levied a tax called the Central Goods and Services Tax on all intra-State supplies of goods or services or both, except on the supply of alcoholic liquor for human consumption, at such rates not exceeding twenty percent as may be notified by the Government on the recommendations of the Council. The tax shall be collected in such manner as may be prescribed and shall be paid by every taxable person. The Central Government may, on the recommendations of the Council, specify categories of supply of goods or services on which the tax shall be paid on reverse charge basis by the recipient. The Government may also specify a class of registered persons who shall pay tax on the goods or services received from an unregistered supplier.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Composition Levy
content: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Act, a registered person whose aggregate turnover in the preceding financial year did not exceed one crore fifty lakh rupees (seventy-five lakh rupees for special category States) may opt to pay, in lieu of the tax payable by him under Section 9, an amount of tax calculated at the prescribed rate but not exceeding five percent for manufacturers and two-and-a-half percent for restaurants and one percent for other suppliers. A person opting for composition scheme cannot collect tax from the recipient, cannot claim input tax credit, and cannot make inter-State outward supplies. The composition scheme is beneficial for small startups and MSMEs as it reduces compliance burden significantly.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Eligibility and Conditions for Taking Input Tax Credit
content: Every registered person shall be entitled to take credit of input tax charged on any supply of goods or services or both which are used or intended to be used in the course or furtherance of his business. The registered person must be in possession of a tax invoice or debit note, must have received the goods or services, the tax charged must have been actually paid to the Government by the supplier, and the person must have furnished the return under Section 39. Where goods are received in lots or installments, ITC shall be available on receipt of the last lot or installment. ITC must be claimed before the thirtieth day of November following the end of the financial year to which the invoice pertains or before furnishing the annual return, whichever is earlier.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Apportionment of Credit and Blocked Credits
content: Where the goods or services or both are used partly for business purposes and partly for other purposes, the amount of credit shall be restricted to so much of the input tax as is attributable to business purposes. Input tax credit shall not be available in respect of: motor vehicles and conveyances (except when used for specified purposes), food and beverages, outdoor catering, beauty treatment, health services, club memberships, rent-a-cab, life insurance, health insurance (unless statutorily obligatory), travel benefits for employees on vacation, works contract services for construction of immovable property, and goods or services received by a non-resident taxable person except on goods imported by him. ITC is also blocked on goods or services used for personal consumption and on goods lost, stolen, destroyed, or disposed of by way of gift.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Persons Liable for Registration
content: Every supplier shall be liable to be registered under this Act in the State or Union territory from where he makes a taxable supply of goods or services or both, if his aggregate turnover in a financial year exceeds twenty lakh rupees (ten lakh rupees for special category States as specified). The threshold was increased to forty lakh rupees for suppliers of goods and twenty lakh rupees for suppliers of services by notification. Every person who is registered or holds a licence under an existing law shall be liable to be registered under this Act. The registration under GST is PAN-based and State-specific, meaning a person operating in multiple States must obtain separate registration in each State.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Compulsory Registration in Certain Cases
content: Notwithstanding anything contained in Section 22, the following categories of persons shall be required to be registered regardless of threshold: persons making inter-State taxable supply; casual taxable persons; persons required to pay tax under reverse charge; persons required to pay tax under Section 9(5) (electronic commerce operators); non-resident taxable persons; persons required to deduct tax under Section 51; persons who supply goods through electronic commerce operators; every electronic commerce operator; persons supplying online information and database access or retrieval services from outside India; and any other person notified by the Government. This is highly relevant for startups operating through e-commerce platforms.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Tax Invoice
content: A registered person supplying taxable goods shall issue a tax invoice before or at the time of removal of goods for supply to the recipient or delivery of goods or making available thereof to the recipient, showing the description, quantity and value of goods, the tax charged thereon, and such other particulars as may be prescribed. A registered person supplying taxable services shall issue a tax invoice before or after the provision of service but within a prescribed period. The invoice shall be prepared in triplicate in case of supply of goods and in duplicate in case of supply of services. A revised invoice may be issued within one month from the date of issuance of certificate of registration for supplies made during the period from the effective date of registration till the date of issuance.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Furnishing Details of Outward Supplies (GSTR-1)
content: Every registered person, other than an input service distributor, a person paying tax under Section 10, a non-resident taxable person, and a person paying TDS, shall furnish the details of outward supplies of goods or services or both during a tax period on or before the tenth day of the month succeeding the said tax period. The details shall include the invoice-wise details of all inter-State and intra-State supplies made during the month, details of debit notes, credit notes, and revised invoices. Quarterly filing is permitted for persons with aggregate turnover up to one crore fifty lakh rupees under the QRMP scheme, where monthly details may be furnished through the Invoice Furnishing Facility.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Furnishing of Returns (GSTR-3B)
content: Every registered person, other than an input service distributor or a non-resident taxable person or a person paying tax under Section 10 or a person required to deduct tax under Section 51 or a person required to collect tax under Section 52, shall for every tax period furnish a return in the prescribed form and manner containing the details of outward and inward supplies, input tax credit availed, tax payable, and tax paid. The return shall be furnished on or before the twentieth day of the month succeeding the tax period. For persons with annual turnover up to five crore rupees, the return may be furnished on a quarterly basis. The return cannot be filed unless the full tax due has been paid.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Annual Return (GSTR-9)
content: Every registered person, other than an input service distributor, a person paying tax under Section 10, a casual taxable person, and a non-resident taxable person, shall furnish an annual return for every financial year electronically in the prescribed form on or before the thirty-first day of December following the end of such financial year. The annual return shall include details of all supplies made and received, input tax credit availed and reversed, tax paid, and any other particulars as may be prescribed. Every registered person whose aggregate turnover during a financial year exceeds five crore rupees shall get his accounts audited and furnish a reconciliation statement in GSTR-9C, duly certified, along with the annual return.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Payment of Tax, Interest, Penalty and Other Amounts
content: Every deposit made towards tax, interest, penalty, fee, or any other amount by a person by internet banking or by using credit or debit cards or by National Electronic Fund Transfer or Real Time Gross Settlement or by such other mode as may be prescribed shall be credited to the electronic cash ledger maintained in the prescribed manner. The amount available in the electronic credit ledger may be used for making any payment towards output tax. The amount of input tax credit available in the electronic credit ledger shall first be utilised towards payment of integrated tax, and the balance, if any, may be utilised towards payment of central tax and State tax in that order. Cross-utilisation between CGST and SGST credit is not permitted directly.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Refund of Tax
content: Any person claiming refund of any tax and interest, if any, paid on such tax or any other amount paid by him may make an application for refund within two years from the relevant date. A registered person may claim refund of any unutilised input tax credit at the end of any tax period on account of zero-rated supplies made without payment of tax or on account of inverted duty structure where the rate of tax on inputs is higher than the rate of tax on output supplies. No refund of unutilised input tax credit shall be allowed if the goods exported out of India are subjected to export duty. The refund shall be processed within sixty days from the date of receipt of the complete application.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Determination of Tax Not Paid or Short Paid or Erroneously Refunded or Input Tax Credit Wrongly Availed or Utilised for Any Reason Other Than Fraud
content: Where it appears to the proper officer that any tax has not been paid or has been short paid or erroneously refunded, or where input tax credit has been wrongly availed or utilised for any reason other than fraud or wilful misstatement or suppression of facts, the proper officer shall serve notice on the person requiring him to show cause as to why he should not pay the amount specified in the notice along with interest and penalty. The notice must be issued at least three months prior to the time limit for issuance of the order. The order shall be issued within three years from the due date for furnishing the annual return for the financial year to which the tax relates. If the person pays the tax along with interest before issuance of notice, no penalty shall be payable and no notice shall be served.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Determination of Tax Not Paid by Reason of Fraud, Wilful Misstatement or Suppression
content: Where it appears to the proper officer that any tax has not been paid or has been short paid or erroneously refunded, or where input tax credit has been wrongly availed or utilised by reason of fraud, wilful misstatement, or suppression of facts to evade tax, the proper officer shall serve a notice requiring the person to show cause. The notice must be issued at least six months prior to the time limit for passing the order. The order must be passed within five years from the due date for furnishing the annual return. The person shall also be liable to pay a penalty equal to the tax so determined. If the person pays the tax, interest, and a penalty of fifteen percent before issuance of the notice, all proceedings shall be deemed to be concluded.

act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
title: Penalty for Certain Offences
content: Where a taxable person who supplies any goods or services without issue of any invoice or issues an incorrect or false invoice, or collects any amount as tax but fails to pay the same to the Government, or collects any tax in contravention of the provisions, or takes or distributes input tax credit in contravention, or fraudulently obtains refund, or furnishes false information, such person shall be liable to pay a penalty of ten thousand rupees or an amount equivalent to the tax evaded or the tax not deducted or short deducted or deducted but not paid or input tax credit availed of or distributed irregularly or the refund claimed fraudulently, whichever is higher. Any person who aids or abets any of the offences shall also be liable to a penalty up to twenty-five thousand rupees.
